user.title: Device Sentinel
user.description: Device Sentinel installs with working defaults and starts watching immediately. Tuning lives in the options after setup.

abort.single_instance_allowed: Device Sentinel is already configured. Only a single instance is supported.

init.title: Device Sentinel Tuning
init.description:
Device Sentinel's default settings are good to get you started. When you begin tailoring the integration to your system, work from top to bottom. If you want alerts to reach your phone, begin in Notifications.

**Notifications**: where alerts go, quiet hours, and the daily reminder.

**Global Exclusions**: hardware you never want judged or reported, by anything. To silence just one kind of report for a device, use that section's own exclude list instead.

**Low Battery**: the low threshold, and battery-only excludes.

**Signal Strength**: how weakening radio links are judged. Coming; learning already runs.

**Freeze Detection**: how long a device may go quiet before it is called frozen, set by two grace sliders. Now running.

**Recovery**: experimental and off by default. Attempts to revive frozen or offline devices.

notifications.title: Notifications and daily brief
notifications.description: Who hears about a problem, and when. [Notification behavior is documented in the wiki.]({wiki_link})

data_description.high_priority_targets: Sent a message when a device develops a problem, and the daily brief with its history.
data_description.normal_priority_targets: Sent the daily brief only, listing what needs attention now.
data_description.persistent_enabled: Shows one card per troubled device on the dashboard.

data_description.quiet_hours_enabled: Nothing is sent during these hours. The daily brief reports what happened.

data_description.daily_reminder_mode: The brief file is written either way; this decides whether it is sent.
data_description.brief_document_targets: Email or other services that can carry the whole document.

exclusions.title: Global Exclusions

data_description.excluded_labels: Devices and entities carrying these labels are excluded. This is the one exclusion you can set without opening this dialog: tag the device or entity from its own page.
data_description.excluded_integrations: Excludes every device the integration owns. Hardware it merely sees but does not own is not caught: a router tracking a camera does not drag the camera with it.

battery.title: Low Battery

data_description.low_threshold: A battery at or below this level is flagged low.
data_description.battery_excluded_integrations: One tick covers every battery that integration owns (phones under mobile_app, for example).
data_description.battery_excluded_labels: Any device carrying one of these labels stops being reported for a low battery. Set the label on the device's own page and it applies here without opening this dialog.
data_description.battery_excluded_devices: Every listed device has a tracked battery. Checked devices are excluded from battery reporting.

signal.title: Signal strength
signal.description: How sharply weakening radio links are flagged, and which devices are left out of signal reporting. The goal: some of your devices should show small readings in the 0-5 percent range, so a real problem stands out as big and obvious. Changes apply to readings going forward; a full day shows their true effect. [How signal strength is judged]({wiki_link}).

data_description.signal_sensitivity: Left is less sensitive: fewer, only-severe flags. Right is more sensitive: weak moments register sooner.
data_description.signal_excluded_integrations: Every device from a ticked integration stops being reported for signal.
data_description.signal_excluded_labels: Devices carrying a ticked label stop being reported for signal.
data_description.signal_excluded_devices: A ticked device stops being reported for signal. It keeps recording, so re-including it brings its history back instantly.

freeze.title: Freeze detection
freeze.description: How long a device may go silent before it is called frozen. Each device is judged against its own learned rhythm, so you are not setting a single timeout; you are setting the grace on top of each device's normal. Delta low is the grace a fast device gets, delta high the grace a slow one gets, and the curve between them is shaped by the two. Raise delta low if a chatty device is flagged too soon; lower delta high if a slow device should be caught faster. [How freeze detection works]({wiki_link}).

data_description.freeze_delta_low: The grace a fast device gets, in minutes: how long past its normal it may go quiet before it counts as frozen.
data_description.freeze_delta_high: The grace a slow device gets, in hours. The two deltas shape the whole curve between them, so a mid-fleet device sits between these.
data_description.freeze_excluded_integrations: Every device from a ticked integration stops being called frozen, unavailable, or not reported.
data_description.freeze_excluded_labels: Devices carrying a ticked label stop being called down. Set the label on the device's own page and it applies here.
data_description.freeze_excluded_devices: A ticked device is never called down. It keeps its clock and rhythm, so re-including it is instant.

advanced.title: Advanced
advanced.description: Settings most people never need to change. [Each one is documented in the wiki.]({wiki_link})

data_description.settle_share_pct: How much of a device's patience must pass before its problem is sent. Higher means fewer messages about brief hiccups.
data_description.episode_share_pct: How quiet a device must be before its silence is recorded for later study. Lower records more, including silences that recover on their own.
data_description.storage_write_minutes: How long routine activity is held before saving, in minutes. Longer is kinder to SD cards; shorter loses less in a power cut.

options.daily: Every day, with an all-clear when nothing needs attention
